raw_consumer_statement:
After multiple attempts to resolve a vendor issue ( failed to provide merchandise, or refund ) I finally submitted a dispute of the charges with CITIBANK on XX/XX/XXXX ; CITI Dispute ID # XXXX Vendor : XXXX XXXX Order # XXXX Amount : {$260.00} Date : XX/XX/XXXX CITIBANK initially reversed the charges to investigate my dispute. On XX/XX/XXXX CITI re-applied the charges explaining that the vendor had shipped the items. No contact information was included. No tracking number was provided. NO merchandise ( clothing ) has been delivered. 

On XX/XX/XXXX I electronically submitted additional details outlining multiple email exchanges with XXXX at XXXXXXXX XXXX ATTN : Manager Dispute Resolutions. I also mailed ( USPS ) a duplicate hard copy file of the emails and the letter and the additional details required on XX/XX/XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX I received notices from CITI that they had received our dispute details and that the case was still open. 

Meanwhile, Ive been requesting to escalate my complaint to a CITI manager. It seems that a communication firewall has been erected around CITI managers, and/or others responsible for carrying out CITIBANKs advertised purchase protections. 

XX/XX/XXXX -The height of absurdity CITI has now closed my dispute by stating that the vendor does not provide refunds Still, no manager names, or contact info provided.
